sanskrit:
स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा।
सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामान्स्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषतः।।
मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्ततः।
transliteration: sa niścayena yoktavyo yogo ’nirviṇṇa-cetasā / saṅkalpa-prabhavān kāmāṃs tyaktvā sarvān aśeṣataḥ // manasaivendriya-grāmaṃ viniyamya samantataḥ
literal_meaning: That yoga is to be practiced with resolute determination and an undeviating mind; having abandoned without remainder all desires born of mental constructs, one must fully restrain the entire group of senses everywhere by the mind alone.
contextual_meaning: Krishna prescribes the inner discipline required for sustained meditative practice: total commitment plus the deliberate relinquishment of every ego-generated craving, followed by a top-down regulation of the senses through conscious attention.
psychological_state: A state of disciplined, non-reactive awareness anchored by clear intention and free from compulsive desire.
trigger_scenario: Deciding to stay off social media for 30 days to reclaim attention, noticing every urge to scroll and gently returning focus to chosen work.
anchor_text: Sit, let every desire go, and rule your senses with steady mind.
